reproduction_summary: Reproduced the paper baseline. Run success: True. Rubric score: 0.696 (target: 0.600).

areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper

leaves.label: The code implements All-CNN-C exactly as derived from base model C: every layer is a 3x3 convolution with ReLU activation sigma(x)=max(x,0)
leaves.why: models.py:AllCNN_C uses only 3x3 Conv2d+ReLU(inplace) blocks and replaces each pool with Conv2d(96,96,k=3,stride=2) and Conv2d(192,192,k=3,stride=2) — output channels equal input channels, matching Table 2's replacement option 2.

leaves.label: All three derived variants of Section 3.1 / Table 2 are implemented and distinguishable in code: Strided-CNN-C (max-pooling removed and the
leaves.why: models.py implements StridedCNN_C (second 3x3 conv stride raised to 2, pools removed), ConvPoolCNN_C (extra 3x3 conv inserted before each MaxPool2d(3,2)), and AllCNN_C (pool replaced by 3x3 stride-2 conv) as distinct classes, and all three trained as separate cells in metrics.jso

leaves.label: Every network ends with a 1-by-1 convolution producing 10 class outputs (CIFAR-10), followed by global averaging over all spatial positions
leaves.why: models.py:_classifier_head ends with Conv2d(192,num_classes,kernel_size=1) → AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1) → Flatten with raw logits for CrossEntropyLoss (softmax in loss); no nn.Linear layer exists anywhere in models.py.

leaves.label: Base models A, B, and C are implemented per Table 1: model B is the Network-in-Network variant with exactly one 1-by-1 convolution after eac
leaves.why: models.py:ModelB has exactly one 1x1 conv (96 and 192) after each 5x5 conv per Table 1, and ModelC uses paired 3x3 convs (96,96 / 192,192) replacing the 5x5s per Table 1; ModelA matches the base A design — all three are trained in metrics.json (a_base, b_base, c_base).

leaves.label: The guided backpropagation visualization of Section 4 / Figure 1 is implemented with the exact masking rule: when propagating back through a
leaves.why: The code listing (models.py, preprocess.py, train scaffolding, per-cell outputs) contains no ReLU-masking visualization code or saliency artifacts at all.

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 (Krizhevsky & Hinton, 2009) are loaded with their standard train/test splits, and all images are whitened and contras
leaves.why: preprocess.py:preprocess_cifar implements GCN (per-image mean/std) + ZCA whitening (epsilon=0.1, Goodfellow-style) applied to the standard torchvision CIFAR-10 train/test splits (cifar_zca_stats.npz cached per cell), but the CIFAR-100 half of the requirement is entirely absent fr

leaves.label: Data augmentation, when used, consists only of adding horizontally flipped examples of all images and randomly translated versions with a ma
leaves.why: preprocess.py:WhitenedDataset applies only transforms.RandomCrop(32, padding=5) (≤5-pixel translation per dimension) and RandomHorizontalFlip when augment=True, with no other augmentations defined anywhere; the leaf's 'when used' condition is met by this pipeline.

leaves.label: The ImageNet experiment uses the ILSVRC-2012 subset of ImageNet for training, and validation images are evaluated only on the center 224x224
leaves.why: The evidence contains no ImageNet experiment at all (all 12 metrics.json cells are dataset=cifar10).

leaves.label: CIFAR training runs use vanilla SGD with fixed momentum 0.9, a total of 350 epochs, the learning-rate schedule S=[200,250,300] with gamma mu
leaves.why: metrics.json shows epochs_run=350 for all 12 cells and best_lr values drawn exactly from {0.25, 0.1, 0.05, 0.01} per model (e.g. a_base best_lr=0.01, c_allcnn best_lr=0.25), and steps_run=136850 = 350×391 confirms full-length runs; the momentum/schedule values are the one unconfi

leaves.label: All CIFAR models are regularized exactly as in Section 3.2: dropout probability 20% on the input image, dropout 50% after each pooling layer
leaves.why: models.py confirms Dropout(p=0.2) on the input of every architecture and Dropout(p=0.5) after each pooling layer or pool-replacement (block-2 dropout plus _classifier_head's leading Dropout(0.5)), exactly per Section 3.2; only the weight-decay value is unverifiable.

leaves.label: Executed run artifacts (training logs, checkpoints, or per-run metrics files) exist for the CIFAR-10 ablation of Table 3 covering base model
leaves.why: All 12 Table-3 cells (a/b/c × base/strided/convpool/allcnn) appear in metrics.json with status ok, epochs_run=350, steps_run=136850, and per-cell output dirs containing .log, metrics.json, training_curves.json, provenance.json, config_used.json, and fig_training.png — the grid wa

leaves.label: The CIFAR-100 experiment reuses the All-CNN-C model with all hyperparameters (learning rate and its schedule) kept fixed from the CIFAR-10 s
leaves.why: The mandatory CIFAR-100 All-CNN-C run is entirely absent from the measured artifacts (the ImageNet clause is conditional and also unattempted).

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 evaluation reports test-set classification error (%) without augmentation for each base and derived model, reporting only the resul
leaves.why: metrics.json reports test_error_pct without augmentation (augment:false) for each of the 12 base and derived models, each with a per-model best_lr selected from the fixed set {0.25, 0.1, 0.05, 0.01} (e.g. a_base 12.86%@lr=0.01, c_base 9.99%@lr=0.1), matching the Table 3 reporting

leaves.label: Separate with-augmentation and without-augmentation CIFAR-10 test-error numbers are produced for All-CNN-C (the All-CNN adapted from base mo
leaves.why: metrics.json c_allcnn.cifar10_noaug shows test_accuracy=0.1/test_error_pct=90.0 and no augmented counterpart cell exists, so neither a valid no-aug number nor any aug number was produced.

leaves.label: If the ImageNet experiment is run, the metric computed is Top-1 validation error on ILSVRC-2012 evaluated only on the center 224x224 patch,
leaves.why: Vacuously satisfied: metrics.json contains only CIFAR-10 cells and no ImageNet experiment was run, so the conditional center-crop Top-1 protocol requirement does not apply.

leaves.label: Model parameter counts are computed and reported (in millions) for the compared models, allowing verification of the paper's claims that the
leaves.why: models.py defines count_parameters() and a __main__ block that would print per-model param counts, but no measured artifact (metrics.json, provenance, logs) reports parameter counts in millions for the compared models.

areas.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

leaves.label: Reproduced base model A achieves approximately 12.5% test error on CIFAR-10 without augmentation, matching the result reported in Section 3.
leaves.why: metrics.json per_model.a_base.cifar10_noaug.base.test_error_pct = 12.86 after 350 epochs (best_lr=0.01), which matches the paper's ~12.5% Table 3 target within reasonable reproduction tolerance.

leaves.label: The reproduced Table 3 ablation reproduces the paper's three qualitative trends from Section 3.2: (1) Strided-CNN variants (pooling removed,
leaves.why: Only trend (1) holds directionally in metrics.json (strided worse than base: 64.82>12.86, 13.27>10.77, 10.52>9.99), while the central All-CNN claims are inverted by chance-level (0.1 accuracy) measured results for all allcnn and convpool cells.

leaves.label: Reproduced All-CNN-C test error on CIFAR-10 is competitive with the paper's Table 4 comparison — i.e., it outperforms the cited prior result
leaves.why: metrics.json per_model.c_allcnn.cifar10_noaug.allcnn shows a dead network at 90% test error, directly contradicting the claim that All-CNN-C outperforms the cited prior results.

leaves.label: If the ImageNet experiment is run, the upscaled All-CNN-B achieves Top-1 validation error close to the reported 41.2% on ILSVRC-2012 (center
leaves.why: The requirement is conditional on the ImageNet experiment being run; metrics.json contains only cifar10_noaug cells (12 a/b/c × variant entries) and the outputs/ listing shows only CIFAR-10 data, so the ImageNet experiment was not run and the conditional is vacuously satisfied.

leaves.label: The repository contains explicit architecture definitions (config files or model-building code) for all models of Table 1 (base A, B, C) and
leaves.why: code/models.py explicitly defines all Table 1 / Table 2 architectures (ModelA/ModelB/ModelC, StridedCNN_C, ConvPoolCNN_C, AllCNN_C — plus all other variants) with a get_model factory, ImageNet was not attempted (so Table 6 is not required), and training scripts are evidenced by _

leaves.label: Every reported number is traceable to a produced artifact: per-model training logs and a metrics file (e.g., metrics.json or equivalent) rec
leaves.why: Every reproduced entry in metrics.json carries test_error_pct/test_accuracy plus the chosen learning rate (best_lr, e.g. a_base best_lr=0.01, c_strided best_lr=0.05), and the outputs/ tree shows per-cell <cell>.log, metrics.json, config_used.json, training_curves.json and provena

leaves.label: If the visualization analysis of Section 4 is reproduced, the repository contains the generated visualization images comparing backpropagati
leaves.why: The requirement is conditional on the Section 4 visualization analysis being reproduced; the code listing and per-cell outputs contain no visualization code or guided-backprop/deconvnet images (only fig_training.png training curves per cell), so the visualization analysis was not

leaf_scores.justification: models.py defines count_parameters() and a __main__ block that would print per-model param counts, but no measured artifact (metrics.json, provenance, logs) reports parameter counts in millions for the compared models.
metrics.json per_model.a_base.cifar10_noaug.base.test_error_pct = 12.86 after 350 epochs (best_lr=0.01), which matches the paper's ~12.5% Table 3 target within reasonable reproduction tolerance.

Only trend (1) holds directionally in metrics.json (strided worse than base: 64.82>12.86, 13.27>10.77, 10.52>9.99), while the central All-CNN claims are inverted by chance-level (0.1 accuracy) measured results for all allcnn and convpool cells.
metrics.json per_model.c_allcnn.cifar10_noaug.allcnn shows a dead network at 90% test error, directly contradicting the claim that All-CNN-C outperforms the cited prior results.

The requirement is conditional on the ImageNet experiment being run; metrics.json contains only cifar10_noaug cells (12 a/b/c × variant entries) and the outputs/ listing shows only CIFAR-10 data, so the ImageNet experiment was not run and the conditional is vacuously satisfied.

code/models.py explicitly defines all Table 1 / Table 2 architectures (ModelA/ModelB/ModelC, StridedCNN_C, ConvPoolCNN_C, AllCNN_C — plus all other variants) with a get_model factory, ImageNet was not attempted (so Table 6 is not required), and training scripts are evidenced by __pycache__/train.cpython-38.pyc and train_cell.cpython-38.pyc together with gpu_cell_runner.py which launches the train_cell.py per-cell trainer.

Every reproduced entry in metrics.json carries test_error_pct/test_accuracy plus the chosen learning rate (best_lr, e.g. a_base best_lr=0.01, c_strided best_lr=0.05), and the outputs/ tree shows per-cell <cell>.log, metrics.json, config_used.json, training_curves.json and provenance.json for each of the 12 cells, making each reported number traceable to artifacts.

The requirement is conditional on the Section 4 visualization analysis being reproduced; the code listing and per-cell outputs contain no visualization code or guided-backprop/deconvnet images (only fig_training.png training curves per cell), so the visualization analysis was not attempted and the conditional is vacuously satisfied.

models.py:AllCNN_C uses only 3x3 Conv2d+ReLU(inplace) blocks and replaces each pool with Conv2d(96,96,k=3,stride=2) and Conv2d(192,192,k=3,stride=2) — output channels equal input channels, matching Table 2's replacement option 2.

models.py implements StridedCNN_C (second 3x3 conv stride raised to 2, pools removed), ConvPoolCNN_C (extra 3x3 conv inserted before each MaxPool2d(3,2)), and AllCNN_C (pool replaced by 3x3 stride-2 conv) as distinct classes, and all three trained as separate cells in metrics.json (c_strided, c_convpool, c_allcnn).

models.py:_classifier_head ends with Conv2d(192,num_classes,kernel_size=1) → AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1) → Flatten with raw logits for CrossEntropyLoss (softmax in loss); no nn.Linear layer exists anywhere in models.py.

models.py:ModelB has exactly one 1x1 conv (96 and 192) after each 5x5 conv per Table 1, and ModelC uses paired 3x3 convs (96,96 / 192,192) replacing the 5x5s per Table 1; ModelA matches the base A design — all three are trained in metrics.json (a_base, b_base, c_base).
The code listing (models.py, preprocess.py, train scaffolding, per-cell outputs) contains no ReLU-masking visualization code or saliency artifacts at all.

preprocess.py:preprocess_cifar implements GCN (per-image mean/std) + ZCA whitening (epsilon=0.1, Goodfellow-style) applied to the standard torchvision CIFAR-10 train/test splits (cifar_zca_stats.npz cached per cell), but the CIFAR-100 half of the requirement is entirely absent from the run.

preprocess.py:WhitenedDataset applies only transforms.RandomCrop(32, padding=5) (≤5-pixel translation per dimension) and RandomHorizontalFlip when augment=True, with no other augmentations defined anywhere; the leaf's 'when used' condition is met by this pipeline.
The evidence contains no ImageNet experiment at all (all 12 metrics.json cells are dataset=cifar10).

metrics.json shows epochs_run=350 for all 12 cells and best_lr values drawn exactly from {0.25, 0.1, 0.05, 0.01} per model (e.g. a_base best_lr=0.01, c_allcnn best_lr=0.25), and steps_run=136850 = 350×391 confirms full-length runs; the momentum/schedule values are the one unconfirmed detail.

models.py confirms Dropout(p=0.2) on the input of every architecture and Dropout(p=0.5) after each pooling layer or pool-replacement (block-2 dropout plus _classifier_head's leading Dropout(0.5)), exactly per Section 3.2; only the weight-decay value is unverifiable.

All 12 Table-3 cells (a/b/c × base/strided/convpool/allcnn) appear in metrics.json with status ok, epochs_run=350, steps_run=136850, and per-cell output dirs containing .log, metrics.json, training_curves.json, provenance.json, config_used.json, and fig_training.png — the grid was actually trained, not just defined.
The mandatory CIFAR-100 All-CNN-C run is entirely absent from the measured artifacts (the ImageNet clause is conditional and also unattempted).

metrics.json reports test_error_pct without augmentation (augment:false) for each of the 12 base and derived models, each with a per-model best_lr selected from the fixed set {0.25, 0.1, 0.05, 0.01} (e.g. a_base 12.86%@lr=0.01, c_base 9.99%@lr=0.1), matching the Table 3 reporting protocol.
metrics.json c_allcnn.cifar10_noaug shows test_accuracy=0.1/test_error_pct=90.0 and no augmented counterpart cell exists, so neither a valid no-aug number nor any aug number was produced.
Vacuously satisfied: metrics.json contains only CIFAR-10 cells and no ImageNet experiment was run, so the conditional center-crop Top-1 protocol requirement does not apply.

weak_leaves.justification: metrics.json per_model.c_allcnn.cifar10_noaug.allcnn shows a dead network at 90% test error, directly contradicting the claim that All-CNN-C outperforms the cited prior results.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

The mandatory CIFAR-100 All-CNN-C run is entirely absent from the measured artifacts (the ImageNet clause is conditional and also unattempted).
metrics.json c_allcnn.cifar10_noaug shows test_accuracy=0.1/test_error_pct=90.0 and no augmented counterpart cell exists, so neither a valid no-aug number nor any aug number was produced.

Only trend (1) holds directionally in metrics.json (strided worse than base: 64.82>12.86, 13.27>10.77, 10.52>9.99), while the central All-CNN claims are inverted by chance-level (0.1 accuracy) measured results for all allcnn and convpool cells.

weak_leaves.justification: models.py defines count_parameters() and a __main__ block that would print per-model param counts, but no measured artifact (metrics.json, provenance, logs) reports parameter counts in millions for the compared models.

preprocess.py:preprocess_cifar implements GCN (per-image mean/std) + ZCA whitening (epsilon=0.1, Goodfellow-style) applied to the standard torchvision CIFAR-10 train/test splits (cifar_zca_stats.npz cached per cell), but the CIFAR-100 half of the requirement is entirely absent from the run.

improvements.title: He Init + LR Warmup to Revive Dead All-CNN Variants
Complete CIFAR-100 and Augmented All-CNN-C Protocol Cells
Add Guided Backprop Visualization and Param-Count Artifacts
